generated_oml:
// C2: Acting Components
instance PermanentMagnetSynchronousMotor : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "A permanent magnet synchronous motor that converts electrical current (I_M) into mechanical torque (τ_M)"
]

instance MultiStageGearSystem : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "A multi-stage gear system (gearwheel + cycloidal gearbox) reducing torque (τ_G → τ_out) while increasing rotational speed with low gear ratio for precise motion control"
]

instance SafetyCell : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Restricts working range to [-90°, 90°] and enforces operational limits on speed (|q̇_max| = 75°/s) via Siemens SINUMERIK 840D sl CNC controller"
]

// C3: Physical Sensing Components
instance MotorEncoder : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Position encoder mounted directly on the permanent magnet synchronous motor measuring angular position"
    DTDFVocab:producedData MotorCurrentMQTTStream
]

instance GearboxEncoder : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Secondary encoder mounted behind the gearbox measuring output angle with reduced positional uncertainty"
    DTDFVocab:producedData PositionEncoderMQTTStream
]

instance MotorCurrentSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Sensor measuring electrical current (I_M) from the permanent magnet synchronous motor"
    DTDFVocab:producedData MotorCurrentMQTTStream
]

// C4: Data Transmission
instance MotorCurrentMQTTStream : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom MotorCurrentSensor
]

instance PositionEncoderMQTTStream : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom MotorEncoder, GearboxEncoder
]

instance FrictionModelOutputMQTTStream : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom MotorCurrentMQTTStream, PositionEncoderMQTTStream
]

// C10: Models/Data
instance StructuralSysMLBDD : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "SysML Block Definition Diagram describing motor → gearbox → cycloidal gearbox system with rotational (torque/velocity) and electric (current) flow definitions"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo MathWorksSimulinkEnvironment
]

instance StructuralSysMLIBD : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "SysML Internal Block Diagram detailing the physical component interactions and interfaces"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo MathWorksSimulinkEnvironment
]

instance IdealGearboxModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Simulation model in MATLAB Simulink representing ideal gearbox behavior"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo MathWorksSimulinkEnvironment
]

instance CoulombViscousFrictionModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Simulation model in MATLAB Simulink implementing Coulomb-Viscous friction behavior"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo MathWorksSimulinkEnvironment
]

instance ReferenceDataTrace : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Measured data including motor current (I_M), output angle, torque, and angular velocity derived from sensors via MQTT"
    DTDFVocab:fromData MotorCurrentMQTTStream, PositionEncoderMQTTStream
]

instance FrictionLossDataTrace : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Simulated friction data (δτ_M, δq̇_M) subtracted from reference values to isolate friction effects"
    DTDFVocab:fromData FrictionModelOutputMQTTStream
]

instance MetadataTrace : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Each data point includes source identification (motor encoder, gearbox) and timestamps for traceability"
    DTDFVocab:fromData MotorCurrentMQTTStream, PositionEncoderMQTTStream, FrictionModelOutputMQTTStream
]

// C11: Enablers
instance MathWorksSimulinkEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "MATLAB Simulink environment for simulation modeling (ideal gearbox, friction models)"
    DTDFVocab:enables FrictionAnalysisService, ModelDrivenControlService
]

instance SiemensSINUMERIKController : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Siemens SINUMERIK 840D sl CNC controller enabling separate axis control with position/speed/acceleration inputs and outputs"
    DTDFVocab:enables ControlParameterUpdateService
]

instance DigitalShadowReferenceModel : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Digital Shadow Reference Model framework with three model types (Engineering, Data Calculation, Simulation) and processing component for traceability"
    DTDFVocab:enables FrictionAnalysisService, VisualizationDashboardService
]

// C6: Services
instance FrictionAnalysisService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Real-time multi-physics-aware identification of joint friction using white-box (analytical) and black-box (data-driven) models"
    DTDFVocab:provides FrictionLossDetectionInsight
]

instance VisualizationDashboardService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Unified dashboard displaying live data traces (motor current, torque, angular velocity) with simulated vs. measured outputs"
    DTDFVocab:provides MotionAccuracyImprovementDashboard
]

instance ModelDrivenControlService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Continuous exchange of friction models during operation to adapt to wear, tool changes, or environmental conditions"
    DTDFVocab:provides DynamicFrictionModelUpdate
]

instance ControlParameterUpdateService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Dynamic adjustment of friction model parameters (Coulomb coefficient, Stribeck curve) for improved motion accuracy"
    DTDFVocab:provides ParameterOptimizationAction
]

// C17: Insights/Actions
instance FrictionLossDetectionInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Real-time identification of static, kinetic, and viscous friction using model differences (simulated vs. measured torque/velocity)"
]

instance MotionAccuracyImprovementDashboard : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Visualization of friction-induced positioning errors for tooling changes and maintenance scheduling"
]

instance DynamicFrictionModelUpdate : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Continuous updating of friction models during operation via dynamic exchange pipelines"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance ParameterOptimizationAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Dynamic adjustment of friction model parameters to improve motion accuracy during operation"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

// Environment Definitions
instance RobotSystemEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:contains PermanentMagnetSynchronousMotor, MultiStageGearSystem, SafetyCell,
        MotorEncoder, GearboxEncoder, MotorCurrentSensor, MathWorksSimulinkEnvironment,
        SiemensSINUMERIKController
]

instance MABIMAX100RobotAxis : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:contains StructuralSysMLBDD, StructuralSysMLIBD, IdealGearboxModel,
        CoulombViscousFrictionModel, ReferenceDataTrace, FrictionLossDataTrace,
        MetadataTrace, FrictionAnalysisService, VisualizationDashboardService,
        ModelDrivenControlService, ControlParameterUpdateService
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The twinning time scale operates in **real-time**, leveraging **live data streaming** via MQTT for **continuous monitoring**. The system processes data at **high-frequency intervals** (implied by the need for real-time friction identification), though the exact latency is not specified. The focus is on **sub-second responsiveness** for immediate feedback to control systems."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The DT constellation is structured as:
  - **Centralized Digital Shadow**: A **single digital shadow** for the first axis of the robot, incorporating **live data streams (MQTT)**, **simulation models (Simulink)**, and **visualization dashboards**.
  - **Hierarchical Orchestration**: The digital shadow acts as a **foundational baseline** for a broader **digital twin** of the robotic system, enabling multi-physics awareness and continuous friction modeling across axes."
]

instance life_cycle_stages : DTDFVocab:EvolutionStage [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin operates primarily in the **‘As Operated’ lifecycle stage**, focusing on **real-time friction analysis** during robot operation. Structural models (SysML) describe the **‘As Designed’ phase** (e.g., motor-gearbox assembly), but the paper does not explicitly address the **‘As Manufactured’** or **‘As Operated’** stages beyond live operational data."
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "The fidelity and validity considerations include:
  - **Verification/Validation**: Friction models are validated by comparing **simulated vs. measured data traces** (e.g., torque/velocity deviations) to detect friction losses.
  - **Uncertainty Handling**: The digital shadow accounts for **sensor noise** (e.g., encoder resolution) and **model parameter drift** (e.g., wear-induced changes in friction coefficients).
  - **Traceability**: All data points are linked to their **sources and computational origins** (e.g., motor encoder, Simulink model) via **metadata and processing components**, ensuring reproducibility."
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "The DT technical connection includes:
  - **Data Transmission**: **MQTT protocol** for real-time streaming of sensor data (motor current, encoders) to the digital shadow.
  - **Model Integration**: **MathWorks MATLAB Simulink** for executable simulation models (e.g., Coulomb-Viscous friction) and **SysML** for structural modeling.
  - **Dashboard Visualization**: Implicitly connected via **unified data aggregation** and **real-time processing pipelines** (e.g., traceability links between data traces and models)."
]

instance horizontal_integration : DTDFVocab:HoriIntegration [
    base:desc "The system integrates with **Asset Administration Shell (AAS)** for standardized representation of the robot’s components, enabling potential **cross-axis sharing** of friction data (though not explicitly detailed). Horizontal integration with **IIoT ecosystems** is implied but not specified."
]

instance standardization : DTDFVocab:Standardization [
    base:desc "The standardization includes:
  - **Digital Shadow Reference Model (DSRM)**: Follows the **UML-based Digital Shadow Reference Model** (Fig. 1) for traceability and conformance.
  - **SysML and AAS**: Uses **SysML for structural modeling** and **Asset Administration Shell (AAS)** for industrial IoT interoperability.
  - **MQTT Protocol**: Standardized **publish-subscribe messaging** for real-time data streaming."
]

instance security_and_safety_considerations : DTDFVocab:SecuritySafety [
    base:desc "Security and safety are addressed through:
  - **Real-time data validation**: Comparison of simulated vs. measured data to prevent erroneous control actions.
  - **Remote operation safety**: The digital twin does not execute autonomous actions on the physical robot (passive data processing).
  - **Access controls**: Dashboard visualization likely restricted to authorized personnel for operational oversight."
]
